[2018-03-28 17:59] .bky_1556 (bky): Note to aspiring designers in the room, a fraction of a gold value per turn is still a big deal.
[2018-03-28 18:05] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Pistol Mech: 10. 9:Gold: 2:Blue:. :Health:2. :Blocker:.
Turn: Get :Attack:.
3:Stamina:: Destroy 1:Health: :Blocker: or :Breach: unit.
(1.1): Costs 7:Gold: 2:Blue:.
(f): "Destroy 1:Health: enemy unit that is not behind :Blocker:."
[2018-03-28 18:06] http404error: Depending on what you paid for it, a fraction of a gold per turn can be either great or abysmal 😛
[2018-03-28 18:07] http404error: But it's definitely true that games are decided wholly over a few gold of value in many cases.
[2018-03-28 18:07] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Bring :Breach: emote back.
[2018-03-28 18:07] namington: Doesn't that break SDR?
[2018-03-28 18:07] http404error: Games of Prismata are a rooster egg perched atop the apex of a roof
[2018-03-28 18:07] namington: Cause you don't want a unit to have blocker
[2018-03-28 18:08] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Blocker isn't really in SDR.
[2018-03-28 18:09] namington: I'm pretty sure that's why, say, deadeye has the non-blocking clause
[2018-03-28 18:09] http404error: @Namington I think it means you can target nonblockers if you're breaching
[2018-03-28 18:09] http404error: But uh, I think it's a small violation, yeah
[2018-03-28 18:09] http404error: It's not a big deal though
[2018-03-28 18:10] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Nope, since :Blocker: is not actually SDR.
[2018-03-28 18:10] .bky_1556 (bky): Structor Arm  (6B, 10 supply)  3 Health
Start of turn: gain 1 attack. If you have 3 or more Structor Arms, gain 2 gold. (only gain 2, no matter how many Structor Arms you have)
[2018-03-28 18:10] http404error: I mean, the unit is about the same if it has "Click: snipe an Engineer"
[2018-03-28 18:10] http404error: And that design was already posted a few days ago
[2018-03-28 18:10] http404error: Ooh, I really like Structor Arm
[2018-03-28 18:12] namington: That's interesting yeah
[2018-03-28 18:12] namington: So once you get 3 it's worse than steelsplitter?
[2018-03-28 18:13] darkeisbein: I don't really like it being at 10 supply then
[2018-03-28 18:13] .bky_1556 (bky): give or take breach situations.
[2018-03-28 18:13] darkeisbein: like there is no point in having it at more then 3 supply basically
[2018-03-28 18:13] namington: Gotta get that value vs Apollo
[2018-03-28 18:13] darkeisbein: seems just way to niche
[2018-03-28 18:13] .bky_1556 (bky): Hmm, buffing to 2 gold.
[2018-03-28 18:14] darkeisbein: also probably just a worse splitter tbh like you have to commit hard for a small gain
[2018-03-28 18:14] darkeisbein: whereas splitter being able to block, having offensive finesse etc. is relevant
[2018-03-28 18:15] http404error: I agree that it could compare more favorably to Militia - I think it's decently better than Splitter though
[2018-03-28 18:15] darkeisbein: how about:
Quarry (1 B. 1 supply):
If you have at least 3 steelsplitters at the start of your turn gain 2 :Gold:
[2018-03-28 18:15] http404error: Hey, that's elegant and does about the same thing without the needed extra wording
[2018-03-28 18:15] .bky_1556 (bky): Yeah, it's more expensive, but
[2018-03-28 18:16] .bky_1556 (bky): it has the same "payoff after a couple inefficient buys" feel.
[2018-03-28 18:16] http404error: It could be 6B and be more like Structor Arm but count Steelsplitters instead to fix the cost issue
[2018-03-28 18:17] http404error: (you'd only need two of course)
[2018-03-28 18:17] .bky_1556 (bky): Structor Arm  (6B, 1 supply)  3 Health
Start of turn: gain 1 attack. If you have 2 or more Steelsplitters, gain 1 gold.
[2018-03-28 18:18] darkeisbein: if you want it to not be dead to apollo you can even make it 4 health 😉
[2018-03-28 18:18] .bky_1556 (bky): And if it's legendary we don't need to cap it at 1 gold.
[2018-03-28 18:18] .bky_1556 (bky): Structor Arm  (6B, 1 supply)  4 Health
Start of turn: gain 1 attack. Gain 1 gold for every 3 Steelsplitters you have.
[2018-03-28 18:20] .bky_1556 (bky): As it happens, I like poke odin dynamics more than odin rush dynamics
[2018-03-28 18:21] http404error: Pokedin - gotta split 'em all
[2018-03-28 18:21] darkeisbein: you could also have the effect on a slightly more expensive blastforge
[2018-03-28 18:21] darkeisbein: what does poke mean in Prismata?
[2018-03-28 18:21] http404error: I like the blastforge idea too! Then you have an incentive to get it first
[2018-03-28 18:21] .bky_1556 (bky): poke = build 1 attack to get your opponent to make a defender.
[2018-03-28 18:22] darkeisbein: Quarry (7 :Gold: 1 supply)
Start of turn: gain :Blue: . Gain 2 :Gold: if you have at least 3 steelsplitters.
[2018-03-28 18:22] .bky_1556 (bky): While building up income instead of more attack.
[2018-03-28 18:23] .bky_1556 (bky): Split the sentences for clarity.
[2018-03-28 18:23] .bky_1556 (bky): Start of turn: gain :Blue: . Gain 2 :Gold: if you have at least 3 steelsplitters.
[2018-03-28 18:24] http404error: 7 gold seems hard to fit into an opener

--- Thread (45 messages, 2018-03-28) ---
[2018-03-28 18:24] darkeisbein: maybe 7 gold is a bit to hard yeah
[2018-03-28 18:24] .bky_1556 (bky): You'd use it as your second forge.
[2018-03-28 18:24] .bky_1556 (bky): The first forge would be a normal forge.
[2018-03-28 18:24] darkeisbein: but I didn't want it to be to natural for p1
[2018-03-28 18:24] .bky_1556 (bky): Then once you already have a splitter or two, you buy the quarry.
[2018-03-28 18:25] darkeisbein: so I guess with this you have to ask yourself do I go early forge and skip this? or get it as a second forge later? Or do I delay forge to get Quarry instead?
[2018-03-28 18:25] .bky_1556 (bky): I think it's fairly costed at 7.
[2018-03-28 18:26] http404error: Steelforge would be fairly costed at 5 too, wouldn't it? 😛
[2018-03-28 18:28] .bky_1556 (bky): Funny you should mention that... my accounting amortizes the cost of the first blastforge into the first absorber.
[2018-03-28 18:28] .bky_1556 (bky): So you need to have $5 to buy it, but some of that $5 buys absorb
[2018-03-28 18:28] .bky_1556 (bky): so the B is cheaper than $5/3 for future units.
[2018-03-28 18:29] darkeisbein: so at 6 we would get:
p1: DD > DD > DD1 > DDS > DDS > DDS is that even good?
[2018-03-28 18:29] http404error: Which is sensible but the question is how much should be counted as that?
[2018-03-28 18:29] darkeisbein: like we get 2 extra drones out of it
[2018-03-28 18:29] .bky_1556 (bky): The second B doesn't get such a discount.
[2018-03-28 18:29] darkeisbein: so I guess it's a good opening
[2018-03-28 18:30] .bky_1556 (bky): "how much should be counted as that?" -> I use $2/3 because it feels right, rather than because it's sound.
[2018-03-28 18:30] darkeisbein: p2: DD > D1 > DS > DDS > DDS
[2018-03-28 18:30] darkeisbein: I guess both player have an opening line with it at 6
[2018-03-28 18:31] .bky_1556 (bky): Math says the correct value is "free" if you buy 3 absorb, but I don't trust the math here.
[2018-03-28 18:31] .bky_1556 (bky): Anyway, the point is that (a) BBB is more than 3 times as expensive as B
[2018-03-28 18:32] .bky_1556 (bky): and (b) Steelforge's 4 cost isn't actually cheaper (in a sense) than the first B.
[2018-03-28 18:33] .bky_1556 (bky): Note on amortizing your B cost like this - the discount applies to the *last* B you spend.
[2018-03-28 18:35] http404error: What I'm trying to imply is that I think 6 cost is fine for the above unit
[2018-03-28 18:35] http404error: Maybe it's too auto-buy then? Idk
[2018-03-28 18:35] http404error: Steelforge is pretty autobuy also though, frankly
[2018-03-28 18:36] .bky_1556 (bky): I think you're overvaluing steelforge.
[2018-03-28 18:36] .bky_1556 (bky): I mean, this thing goes great with stealforge if you want BBB
[2018-03-28 18:36] .bky_1556 (bky): but then you end up with BBB.
[2018-03-28 18:37] darkeisbein: I kinda like that it gives p2 a good B opening
[2018-03-28 18:38] darkeisbein: Like DD > D1 > SD is actually pretty good because if your opponent goes for natural Quarry timing you can kill one engineer so you buying less drones doesn't really matter
[2018-03-28 18:39] .bky_1556 (bky): That runs into DD, DD, DDB, DDW and P1 is up 2 drones.
[2018-03-28 18:40] darkeisbein: yeah but you get 2 from Quarry
[2018-03-28 18:41] .bky_1556 (bky): With 6 gold Quarry, they quarry back instead of blastforging.
[2018-03-28 18:41] darkeisbein: yeah but there quarry get's online 1 and a half turn later
[2018-03-28 18:41] darkeisbein: maybe that is still p1 favored?
[2018-03-28 18:43] .bky_1556 (bky): It's still P1 favored - P2 gets the quarry online sooner. This gets them to parity for a turn and a half
[2018-03-28 18:43] .bky_1556 (bky): and then P1's quarry comes online.
[2018-03-28 18:44] darkeisbein: but p2 pumps out attack faster?
[2018-03-28 18:44] .bky_1556 (bky): P2 needs to get their own wall at some point.
[2018-03-28 18:44] .bky_1556 (bky): Probably after the third splitter.
[2018-03-28 18:49] darkeisbein: hm weird line for p2: DD > D1 > DSE > DDD > DDDS
[2018-03-28 18:49] .jrkirby (jrkirby): @bky I'd prefer seeing structor arms with a supply count closer to where the effect kicks in. Why do the other 7 supply exist, from a design standpoint, when it's worse than steelsplitter? Sure, it's more supply once splitter is gone, but that's very boring.
[2018-03-28 18:50] darkeisbein: we already fixed that 😉
[2018-03-28 18:50] .jrkirby (jrkirby): oh :LoganHaa: didn't read enough

[2018-03-28 19:36] .jrkirby (jrkirby): B is less than GG, IMO
[2018-03-28 19:37] .jrkirby (jrkirby): right, lower tech, more gold
[2018-03-28 19:37] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): No, 2Blue is more than 3Green.
[2018-03-28 19:39] .jrkirby (jrkirby): I thought it was 9B, it's 9BB
[2018-03-28 19:41] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): But 7BB may be better.

[2018-03-29 22:59] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Closest thing to a Phoenix type unit.

[2018-03-29 23:03] .bky_1556 (bky): barring Frontline corner cases.
[2018-03-29 23:03] .bky_1556 (bky): So it's pure threat.

[2018-03-29 23:05] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): But if you get breached, you may avoid losing these.

[2018-03-29 23:05] f300xen (f0x): and then you lose tarsiers and lose anyways, so it does basically nothing

[2018-03-29 23:06] darkeisbein: as soon as you have a single tarsier you don't want to get small breached

--- Thread (47 messages, 2018-03-29) ---
[2018-03-29 23:25] .jrkirby (jrkirby): A stream of gauss charges is what, 7E, buildtime 2, gain 1 attack?
[2018-03-29 23:26] .bky_1556 (bky): 8GE if you want the stream right away
[2018-03-29 23:26] f300xen (f0x): i mean it's more like you make a pixie each turn, so it's not really like that
[2018-03-29 23:27] .bky_1556 (bky): The moral of course is that you don't want to just blindly stream your gauss charges. Make a decision each turn based on defender's convenience.
[2018-03-29 23:28] .bky_1556 (bky): Blindly streaming and clicking pixies is even worse.
[2018-03-29 23:29] .jrkirby (jrkirby): The moral of the story is: don't build C specifically for 1 charge per turn. Build it for other stuff, and only buy charges when you can't get the other stuff any more. But this is all #strategy_advice
[2018-03-29 23:31] masn6811 (Masne98888): the moral of the story is that gauss charge needs a buff
[2018-03-29 23:31] masn6811 (Masne98888): the ratio is just so bad
[2018-03-29 23:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): like other units that need a buff (eg. shadowfang) there are sets where you want to use it
[2018-03-29 23:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): well, shadowfang doesn't need a buff as much as goose charge does
[2018-03-29 23:32] .bky_1556 (bky): streaming 2 charges a turn costs you roughly an omega splitter.
[2018-03-29 23:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): but charge doesnt need a buff as much as say thermite
[2018-03-29 23:32] .jrkirby (jrkirby): goose charge doesn't need a buff
[2018-03-29 23:32] .bky_1556 (bky): Or a cynestra if you prefer green stuff.
[2018-03-29 23:34] f300xen (f0x): charge is probably untouchable because it's too weak as is but doing anything to it makes it broken
[2018-03-29 23:35] wilson0862 (Wilson): ^ without increasing the complexity.
[2018-03-29 23:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): make it construct another gauss charge with :Time: 5 or something
[2018-03-29 23:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): or make it give :Attack: :Energy: maybe
[2018-03-29 23:38] .bky_1556 (bky): Charge isn't actually behind the curve, it only looks like it is.
[2018-03-29 23:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): would you rather pay 4 :Gold: or construct a gauss cahrge for your opponent?
[2018-03-29 23:39] .bky_1556 (bky): Usually I'd construct the gauss charge.
[2018-03-29 23:39] .bky_1556 (bky): Occasionally I'd pay.
[2018-03-29 23:40] masn6811 (Masne98888): well, id almost always make the charge
[2018-03-29 23:40] masn6811 (Masne98888): and thus id rather buy bolt than 4 charges
[2018-03-29 23:40] masn6811 (Masne98888): so charge needs a buff
[2018-03-29 23:41] .bky_1556 (bky): Cost-efficiency in Prismata is balanced around unit size. Large units get a bulk discount compared to small units.
[2018-03-29 23:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): true, but this is more so at bigger numbers
[2018-03-29 23:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): a 30 :Gold: unit gets a huge bulk discount compared to a 10
[2018-03-29 23:42] .bky_1556 (bky): Charge looks like it's inefficient, but it's half the bulk, or less, of what you're comparing it to.
[2018-03-29 23:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): a 4 :Green: unit gets a small bult discount compared to a 1 :Green:
[2018-03-29 23:42] .bky_1556 (bky): Its closest competitors are Pixie, which is indeed costed similarly
[2018-03-29 23:42] .bky_1556 (bky): and Immolite, which is about 1.7x its bulk.
[2018-03-29 23:43] masn6811 (Masne98888): well, the reason pixie is fine
[2018-03-29 23:43] masn6811 (Masne98888): is because pixie isn't usually bought for 1 :Blue:
[2018-03-29 23:43] masn6811 (Masne98888): you dont see people massing forges for it, even when there's steelforge in the set
[2018-03-29 23:44] masn6811 (Masne98888): pixie usually just costs 1 :Gold:
[2018-03-29 23:45] .bky_1556 (bky): It's like comparing borehole patroller to shadowfang.
[2018-03-29 23:48] .bky_1556 (bky): Convenience is just as important as efficiency, and Gauss Charge is just about the highest convenience attacker on the market.
[2018-03-29 23:49] .bky_1556 (bky): Hits for 1 on demand whenever the marginal cost of 1 defense more than the cost of the gauss charge.
[2018-03-29 23:49] .bky_1556 (bky): No commitment necessary
[2018-03-29 23:54] .bky_1556 (bky): To the room, the lesson: keep convenience in mind when balancing your units
[2018-03-29 23:54] .bky_1556 (bky): Nitrocybe isn't as convenient of an attacker because it's *not* available on demand.
[2018-03-29 23:54] .bky_1556 (bky): It makes up for that by being able to block.
[2018-03-29 23:55] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): And faster to amass.
[2018-03-29 23:56] wilson0862 (Wilson): But how about to bmass?
[2018-03-29 23:59] darkeisbein: so is a cybe animus 12EE for 2 attack with buildtime 3?
[2018-03-29 23:59] darkeisbein: and some blocking and finesse shenanigans?

[2018-03-30 00:20] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Thermite Core should cost 7B but give attack.
[2018-03-30 00:22] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): And pay 2 Attack for 2 Pixies.
[2018-03-30 00:22] iminabearsuit: Oh... I was just about to say, it was a very reasonable change. Until you changed the click ability.
[2018-03-30 00:24] iminabearsuit: I mean, as a unit. It would be reasonable. But it's moving quite far away from the feel of the unit.
[2018-03-30 00:24] iminabearsuit: Like a lifespan milita that made pixies instead of gold, would be entirely reasonable unit.
[2018-03-30 00:25] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Then 6B for added Attack?
[2018-03-30 00:26] iminabearsuit: It would be quite similar to Militia. But I think 6B, for lifespan 8 attacker. With thermite clicks, would be reasonable.-
[2018-03-30 00:28] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Militia is pretty weak.
[2018-03-30 00:29] iminabearsuit: It's not a set defining unit, but it's fine.
[2018-03-30 00:29] f300xen (f0x): many things are worse than tarsier, but you buy them anyways
[2018-03-30 00:29] iminabearsuit: Every random set unit can't be ematrix or centurion
[2018-03-30 00:29] masn6811 (Masne98888): centurion needs a nerf tho
[2018-03-30 00:30] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Militia Click should give 1e.
[2018-03-30 00:30] iminabearsuit: Seems really irrelevant, and needlesly confusing.
[2018-03-30 00:33] iminabearsuit: Seems like a straight nerf.
[2018-03-30 00:35] iminabearsuit: Like, militia is completly fine in the current state. A lot of the time it's just a steelsplitter that snipes an engi on it's first attack, but can't block.
[2018-03-30 00:36] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): The point being turning it into a xBB unit.
[2018-03-30 00:36] iminabearsuit: Militia is only good when you buy it as your first attacker.
[2018-03-30 00:36] kaelii: BB isn't cheaper. Basically ever?
[2018-03-30 00:36] iminabearsuit: You don't buy BB before your first attacker.
[2018-03-30 00:38] kaelii: 2BB is a sink, and you probably never go militia unless there is something else at BB or BBB but you're buffing those BB/BBB units in sets with militia by a fairly large amount.
[2018-03-30 00:38] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): DBB would be viable.
[2018-03-30 00:39] kaelii: I feel like you're underestimating the cost of 2 blastforges in not already blue sets.
[2018-03-30 00:39] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Compensates for BB clunk.
[2018-03-30 00:39] kaelii: And I don't think you could reasonably tweak militia's cost to _BB such that people would go BB for militia.
[2018-03-30 00:40] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): But that's not quite the point.
[2018-03-30 00:40] kaelii: Unless you tweak what militia does.
[2018-03-30 00:40] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Militia's point is finesse.
[2018-03-30 00:40] iminabearsuit: Militia is a unit designed for it's click ability. Not for being an attacker. A lot of players first experience with exploiting comes from Militia, and I think the unit in it's current state is in the absolute best version for teaching players that.
[2018-03-30 00:42] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Ok, but 3BD would still keep that?
Seems just complex.
[2018-03-30 00:43] iminabearsuit: No, the current version is perfect. Since it has the same cost as steelsplitter. So it makes new players directly compare them, and think of the applications where the other is better than the other.
[2018-03-30 00:43] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Buy back Drone, or don't.
[2018-03-30 00:44] iminabearsuit: Also 3BD would be garbage for the unit. You want militias early, but at that cost, it's a unit you buy when you have a hard time defending, at which point the militia clicks become bad.
[2018-03-30 00:45] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Buy drone back then.
[2018-03-30 00:45] f300xen (f0x): it'd let you finally go monoblue breachproof
[2018-03-30 00:45] f300xen (f0x): which is of course a terrible idea
[2018-03-30 00:46] kaelii: The other risk at that cost is possibly opening up some really questionable rushes -- like maybe I DO go BB and convert 10 drones into militia while maintaining my economy.
[2018-03-30 00:46] f300xen (f0x): drone build cost is a green/neutral identity, not a blue one

[2018-03-30 00:51] http404error: it's kind of like ~4 doomed drones for the price of ~2?
[2018-03-30 00:52] kaelii: Seems like a good buy if you can identify the range where you go for finalish purchases
[2018-03-30 00:53] f300xen (f0x): it sort of seems like shitty ebb but idk
[2018-03-30 00:53] http404error: I'm the kind of player that enjoys letting my opponent go for this kind of thing and trying to outlast them

[2018-03-30 00:53] kaelii: Its 15 more gold than you would otherwise have on N+3 if you didn't buy it, +6 by N+4 and -3 by N+5
